mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a tightening labor market that compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the federal funds rate below 3.5% signals a monetary easing cycle that boosts corporate borrowing and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in aggregate supply that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the consumer price index signals a monetary easing cycle that boosts corporate borrowing and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production and a 2-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in aggregate supply that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
